pages.full_text:
[Photo of mosaic scene]
Death of the Righteous Man

[Printed text block — The floor mosaic]
The restoration of 1863 revealed in the presbytery, a large portion of the Romanesque mosaic floor (Fig. 14) from the first half of the twelfth century. In the middle of a sequence of arcs, the figure of the Year (King) on the throne is flanked on the left by the months of February (churning), March (blowing the horns) and April (holding flowers) and on the right, by May [illegible] (with the sickle), June and July (reap grain). Below is a portion of a labyrinth organized in geometric concentric arcs.

[Photo of mosaic floor with labyrinth and calendar scenes]
St Michael
Major church

[Vertical text in right margin:] Wooden model of Pavia Cathedral

the Visconti Family. There is a large archeological museum + art gallery. Romanesque sculpture. V. stuffy rooms. A huge enclosed area. [?14]. We cycled back + most of the washing was dry.
We swam.
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. The page contains two photographs of mosaic artwork, one with a printed caption block about 'The floor mosaic' (appears to be a cut-out from a guidebook or postcard). The handwritten caption 'Death of the Righteous Man' is below the first photo. 'St Michael / Major church' appears handwritten to the right of the second photo. The vertical text 'Wooden model of Pavia Cathedral' in the right margin continues onto the right page. The text at the bottom is a continuation from a previous page — begins mid-sentence with 'the Visconti Family'. '[?14]' could be a date (the 14th) or a page/figure reference. 'archeological' is transcribed as written (diarist's spelling). The church 'St Michael Major' (San Michele Maggiore) is a famous Romanesque church in Pavia, Italy. The Visconti family reference and Pavia Cathedral place this firmly in Pavia, Italy.

pages.full_text:
[Vertical text on left side:] Wooden model of Pavia Cathedral, kept in the Castle. It was made because it was more accurate for making the building than drawings would be. It's huge. It might just fit in our kitchen.

[Large photo of wooden model of Pavia Cathedral]

__Friday__ We packed up + 6 st. In the night the fridge kept going on + off so we switched it off. We think it's because there were so many trees, shade, in the campsite. The fridge uses 40w.
photo_captions: Wooden model of Pavia Cathedral, kept in the Castle. It was made because it was more accurate for making the building than drawings would be. It's huge. It might just fit in our kitchen.
pages.notes: No handwritten page number clearly visible. The top-right corner shows what may be a page number but it is cut off/unclear. The vertical caption text on the left side describes the large photo of the wooden scale model of Pavia Cathedral (Duomo di Pavia), which is housed in the Visconti Castle (Castello Visconteo) museum in Pavia. 'Friday' is underlined as a heading. '6 st.' is unclear — could mean '6 [o'clock] start' or similar; transcribed as written. 'The fridge uses 40w' — '40w' is transcribed as written (40 watts). Year cannot be determined from this page alone; the trip is in Pavia, Italy, consistent with the 2003–2019 date range of volume B.
